commit.message:
🐛 fix: apply strip_self_from_templating_gemfile to all modular gemfiles

Previously, only templating.gemfile had the host-gem self-reference
stripped. But coverage.gemfile (and potentially other modular gemfiles)
also list specific kettle-rb gems as dependencies — e.g. coverage.gemfile
contains `gem "kettle-soup-cover"`. When kettle-soup-cover runs kettle-jem
on itself, its coverage.gemfile must not declare a dependency on itself.

The fix applies strip_self_from_templating_gemfile to every modular gemfile
(both template_content and post-merge content), while keeping
strip_self_from_templating_local scoped to templating_local.gemfile only
(that method handles the specialized %w[] array / VENDORED_GEMS comment
format unique to that file).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#58): reactive engine init — use file existence not WorkManager state

The one-shot engine init in initializeConversation() failed silently when
the preferred model's WorkManager task was still RUNNING or FAILED at startup
(e.g. E4B worker was mid-download when the process was restarted). In that
case downloadStates.value[preferred] == Downloading/Error, not Downloaded,
so the initialize() call was skipped and the app stuck on Loading indefinitely.

Two fixes:
1. Replace one-shot init with initEngineWhenReady() — a reactive coroutine
   that waits on downloadStates until all required models report Downloaded,
   then initialises the engine. Fires immediately if models are already on disk.
2. Use downloadManager.getModelPath() (file-existence check) for the model path
   rather than the StateFlow value, so models pushed manually via ADB are
   recognised regardless of WorkManager task state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 fix: template all modular gemfiles in preflight

Replace narrow BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES list + empty-stub fallback
with a full recursive walk of the installed template directory.

Previously, only templating.gemfile and templating_local.gemfile were
written in the pre-bundled phase; every other modular gemfile received
an empty stub. Empty stubs caused `bundle install` to fail with
`cannot load such file -- rubocop/rake_task` (and similar) when the
Rakefile tried to require gems that were absent from the bundle.

New behaviour:
- preflight_merge_modular_gemfiles! globs all *.gemfile.example files
  recursively under the installed template/gemfiles/modular/ tree.
- Each file is token-resolved and merged (or written fresh) into the
  destination repo before bundle install runs.
- The BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES / BOOTSTRAP_FORCEABLE_MODULAR_GEMFILES
  constants are removed as no longer needed.
- preflight_ensure_eval_gemfile_stubs! and ensure_bootstrap_modular_gemfiles!
  are removed (dead code whose job is now done by the glob-based walk).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: linyuliu <82999749+linyuliu@users.noreply.github.com>

Install python3-ijson from Debian repos for route_check performance (#26633)

PR #4294 forced the pure-Python ijson backend in route_check.py to avoid
yajl2_cffi crashes under monit's MemoryDenyWriteExecute=true. However, the
python backend is slow, causing a regression on scaled platforms (21 min for
100K routes on Nokia chassis).

Root cause: sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models pin ijson==3.2.3 (exact),
forcing pip to download from PyPI even when Debian's python3-ijson (3.4.0,
with the fast yajl2_c C extension) is installed. The PyPI wheel for Python
3.13 lacks the _yajl2 C extension, and pip's copy at /usr/local/lib/ shadows
the Debian package at /usr/lib/python3/.

Fix:
- Relax ijson==3.2.3 to ijson>=3.2.3 in sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models
- Install python3-ijson via apt before pip installs sonic-yang-mgmt, so pip
  sees Debian's 3.4.0 and skips the PyPI download entirely

With this change, ijson auto-detects the yajl2_c backend (24x faster than
the python backend, safe under MemoryDenyWriteExecute).

Fixes: sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#23442

Signed-off-by: Deepak Singhal <deepsinghal@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#56): download worker saves to external storage to match isDownloaded() check

ModelDownloadWorker was saving to context.filesDir (internal storage) but
KernelModel.localFile() and isDownloaded() resolve against
context.getExternalFilesDir("models") (scoped external storage).

This mismatch meant downloaded models were never detected as present,
causing the app to re-download on every launch and show "Loading model..."
indefinitely after a completed download.

Fix: use getExternalFilesDir("models") with filesDir fallback, matching
KernelModel.localFile() exactly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps to fix CI dependency install

@wordpress/scripts requires @wordpress/env ^10.0.0 as a peer
dependency, but we use @wordpress/env ^11.3.0. This causes
npm install to fail with ERESOLVE in CI. Setting
legacy-peer-deps=true resolves the conflict.

* fix: auto-reboot Pico W into BOOTSEL mode for headless flashing

Add force_bootsel() function to flash.sh that uses `picotool reboot -f -u`

press the BOOTSEL button, enabling reliable headless CI flashing.

- Call force_bootsel before flashing device 1 (initial preparation)

- Remove "Please put the second Pico W into BOOTSEL mode now" prompt

* fix: keyboard shortcuts, MCP config refresh, session links, spec accuracy (#125)

* fix: wire keyboard shortcut preferences into runtime bindings

Settings UI already let users remap keyboard shortcuts (stored as
Ctrl+N format in preferences.keyboardShortcuts), but CommandPalette
used hardcoded display-format shortcuts (⌘N) and App.tsx hardcoded
Ctrl+K for the palette toggle. Runtime bindings never consumed the
user's configured preferences.

Changes:
- useKeyboardShortcuts: unified parser handles both settings (Ctrl+X)
  and display (⌘X) formats with full Alt modifier support; exports
  settingsToDisplayShortcut, matchesShortcutConfig, COMMAND_SHORTCUT_MAP
- CommandPalette: reads preferences.keyboardShortcuts to populate each
  command's shortcut dynamically with defensive fallbacks
- App.tsx: palette toggle uses shared matchesShortcutConfig instead of
  duplicated inline parser
- ActivityBar: tooltip shows user-configured palette shortcut
- 19 new tests covering settings format parsing, Alt modifiers,
  display conversion, matchesShortcutConfig, and exact modifier matching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: refresh MCP configs when plugin settings are updated

When plugin settings change via PUT /plugin/{id}/settings, the MCP
config files were not being refreshed — setting variable substitution
in MCP server args/env would remain stale until plugin reload.

Added RefreshMcpConfigsAsync to IPluginHost interface, implemented in
PluginHost (delegates to McpPluginConfigManager.RefreshPluginConfigsAsync),
and called from PluginController.UpdateSettings after settings save.

suites since both components now use react-router hooks.

* fix: align SignalR enum serialization with HTTP API for history replay (#150)

The HTTP API used JsonStringEnumConverter (enums as strings) while SignalR
used default serialization (enums as integers). When the frontend loaded
chat history via GET /sessions/{id}/events on reattach, eventsToMessages()
compared string eventTypes against integer constants — every event was
silently skipped, producing an empty chat window.

Fix: add JsonStringEnumConverter to SignalR's AddJsonProtocol config and
update all frontend enum constants from integers to matching strings.

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

commit.message:
feat(skills): add systematic-literature-review skill for multi-paper SLR workflows (#2032)

* feat(skills): add systematic-literature-review skill for multi-paper SLR workflows

Adds a new skill that produces a structured systematic literature review (SLR)
across multiple academic papers on a topic. Addresses #1862 with a pure skill
approach: no new tools, no architectural changes, no new dependencies.

Skill layout:
- SKILL.md — 4+1 phase workflow (plan, search, extract, synthesize, present)
- scripts/arxiv_search.py — arXiv API client, stdlib only, with a
  requests->urllib fallback shim modeled after github-deep-research's
  github_api.py
- templates/{apa,ieee,bibtex}.md — citation format templates selected
  dynamically in Phase 4, mirroring podcast-generation's templates/ pattern

Design notes:
- Multi-paper synthesis uses the existing `task` tool to dispatch extraction
  subagents in parallel. SKILL.md's Phase 3 includes a fixed decision table
  for batch splitting to respect the runtime's MAX_CONCURRENT_SUBAGENTS = 3
  cap, and explicitly tells the agent to strip the "Task Succeeded. Result: "
  prefix before parsing subagent JSON output.
- arXiv only, by design. Semantic Scholar and PubMed adapters would push the
  scope toward a standalone MCP server (see #933) and are intentionally out
  of scope for this skill.
- Coexists with the existing `academic-paper-review` skill: this skill does
  breadth-first synthesis across many papers, academic-paper-review does
  single-paper peer review. The two are routed via distinct triggers and
  can compose (SLR on many + deep review on 1-2 important ones).
- Hard upper bound of 50 papers, tied to the Phase 3 concurrency strategy.
  Larger surveys degrade in synthesis quality and are better split by
  sub-topic.

BibTeX template explicitly uses @misc for arXiv preprints (not @article),
which is the most common mistake when generating BibTeX for arXiv papers.

arxiv_search.py was smoke-tested end-to-end against the live arXiv API with
two query shapes (relevance sort, submittedDate sort with category filter);
all returned JSON fields parse correctly (id normalization, Atom namespace
handling, URL encoding for multi-word queries).

* fix(skills): prevent LLM from saving intermediate search results to file

Adds an explicit "do not save" instruction at the end of Phase 2.
Observed during Test 1 with DeepSeek: the model saved search results
to a markdown file before proceeding to Phase 3, wasting 2-3 tool call
rounds and increasing the risk of hitting the graph recursion limit.
The search JSON should stay in context for Phase 3, not be persisted.

* fix(skills): use relevance+start-date instead of submittedDate sorting

Test 2 revealed that arXiv's submittedDate sorting returns the most
recently submitted papers in the category regardless of query relevance.
Searching "diffusion models" with sortBy=submittedDate in cs.CV returned
papers on spatial memory, Navier-Stokes, and photon-counting CT — none
about diffusion models. The LLM then retried with 4 different queries,
wasting tool calls and approaching the recursion limit.

Fix: always sort by relevance; when the user wants "recent" papers,
combine relevance sorting with --start-date to constrain the time window.
Also add an explicit "run the search exactly once" instruction to prevent
the retry loop.

* fix(skills): wrap multi-word arXiv queries in double quotes for phrase matching

Without quotes, `all:diffusion model` is parsed by arXiv's Lucene as
`all:diffusion OR model`, pulling in unrelated papers from physics
(thermal diffusion) and other fields. Wrapping in double quotes forces
phrase matching: `all:"diffusion model"`.

Also fixes date filtering: the previous bug caused 2011 papers to appear
in results despite --start-date 2024-04-09, because the unquoted query
words were OR'd with the date constraint.

Verified: "diffusion models" --category cs.CV --start-date 2024-04-09
now returns only relevant diffusion model papers published after April
2024.

* fix(skills): add query phrasing guide and enforce subagent delegation

Two fixes from Test 2 observations with DeepSeek:

1. Query phrasing: add a table showing good vs bad query examples.
   The script wraps multi-word queries in double quotes for phrase
   matching, so long queries like "diffusion models in computer vision"
   return 0 results. Guide the LLM to use 2-3 core keywords + --category
   instead.

2. Subagent enforcement: DeepSeek was extracting metadata inline via
   python -c scripts instead of using the task tool. Strengthen Phase 3
   to explicitly name the task tool, say "do not extract metadata
   yourself", and explain why (token budget, isolation). This is more
   direct than the previous natural-language-only approach while still
   providing the reasoning behind the constraint.

* fix(skills): strengthen search keyword guidance and subagent enforcement

Address two issues found during end-to-end testing with DeepSeek:

1. Search retry: LLM passed full topic descriptions as queries (e.g.
   "diffusion models in computer vision"), which returned 0 results due
   to exact phrase matching and triggered retries. Added explicit
   instruction to extract 2-3 core keywords before searching.

2. Subagent bypass: LLM used python -c to extract metadata instead of
   dispatching via task tool. Added explicit prohibition list (python -c,
   bash scripts, inline extraction) with ❌ markers for clarity.

* fix(skills): address Copilot review feedback on SLR skill

- Fix legacy arXiv ID parsing: preserve archive prefix for pre-2007
  papers (e.g. hep-th/9901001 instead of just 9901001)
- Fix phase count: "four phases" -> "five phases"
- Add subagent_enabled prerequisite note to SKILL.md Notes section

  templates, replace with workflow-scoped wording
- Fix script header: "stdlib only" -> "no additional dependencies
  required", fix relative path to github_api.py reference
- Remove reference to non-existent docs/enhancement/ path in header

* Apply suggestions from code review

fix: three bugs — check-in data loss, button nesting, date hydration

- dance/page.tsx CheckInTab: add useEffect([existing?.id]) to sync
  scores + reflection state when localStorage data arrives after mount.
  Root cause: useState initializes once; on first render checkIns=[]
  so existing=undefined and all scores defaulted to emptyScores().
  After the parent useEffect loaded saved data, props updated but
  local state stayed empty. Fix: sync once when existing.id appears.

- dance/page.tsx History tab: change inner Edit <button> (line 871)
  to <div role='button'> — buttons cannot be descendants of buttons,
  which caused a React hydration error.

- daily/page.tsx: move todayDateStr computation from module level
  into the component via useState('') + useEffect, computed with
  new Date() on the client only. Eliminates the SSR/client date
  mismatch (server rendered Apr 3, client rendered Apr 9).
